ai_scope_discriminator: A Weaviate backup is by definition AI/ML data: every backup-status JSON carries a `classes` array that enumerates the vector COLLECTION names included in the snapshot (each class is a set of objects + their embeddings/vectors). To confirm in-scope (vector data, not generic substrate) without touching object contents: (1) GET /v1/backups/{backend}/{id} and read the `classes[]` field — non-empty class list == vector collections captured. (2) Cross-check against the live schema GET /v1/schema (returns `classes[].vectorizer`, `vectorIndexType` like hnsw/flat, and `properties[]`), and GET /v1/meta `modules` which lists vectorizer modules (text2vec-*, multi2vec-*, generative-*). Presence of a vectorizer module + hnsw index + the same class names in the backup manifest is the gate. A backup with an empty/absent `classes` list or a host whose /v1/schema has zero classes is an empty/non-AI instance and is OUT of scope.
fingerprint_markers: /v1/meta returns JSON with vendor-unique field "grpcMaxMessageSize" alongside "hostname","version","modules"
Root / and /v1 basePath; OpenAPI title "Weaviate" / description "Contains meta information of the current Weaviate instance."
Backup-status JSON keys: id, backend, path, status, classes (status enum STARTED|TRANSFERRING|TRANSFERRED|SUCCESS|FAILED|CANCELED)
fp_guards: Broad http.title:"Weaviate" also catches the Weaviate web console / third-party dashboards and docs mirrors that never expose the API — anchor on the JSON field grpcMaxMessageSize from /v1/meta, never the title.

Port 8080 is a generic HTTP/proxy/Tomcat/k8s-dashboard port; the bare port:8080 match is mostly non-Weaviate. Require the grpcMaxMessageSize JSON marker (Weaviate-unique; not present on Milvus/Qdrant/Chroma).

LBot/catch-all deception hosts echo arbitrary banners — confirm by re-probing /v1/meta for a coherent semver `version` AND /v1/schema returning a real classes[] structure (catch-all returns the same body for any path; a 404 on /v1/nonexistent that differs from /v1/meta is the negative guard).
A 401 on /v1/meta means AUTHENTICATION_ANONYMOUS_ACCESS_ENABLED was set false (API-key/OIDC on) — surface open, access not exercised; do not label unauth.
backup-status 404/"no backup" with backups module disabled is a Weaviate host but NOT a backup-exposure finding — exclude from the backup_snapshot count, keep in the platform count.

cves.desc: Backup restore ZipSlip path traversal (GHSA-7v39-2hx7-7c43): crafted entry name with absolute path or ../ escapes the restore root to write/overwrite arbitrary files in the process scope; CVSS 8.7; PR:None; fixed 1.30.20/1.31.19/1.32.16/1.33.4.

Shard-movement FileReplicationService GetFile arbitrary file read (GHSA pair to 67818): missing fileName validation during 'Pause file activity' lets a caller read any file accessible to the service process; fixed in the same 1.30.20/1.31.19/1.32.16/1.33.4 line.

pivot_paths: GET /v1/meta -> read version (CVE scoping vs 1.33.4 fix line), hostname, modules (which backup-* + vectorizer modules are enabled).
GET /v1/backups/{backend} for backend in {filesystem,s3,gcs,azure} -> LIST backup ids/paths/status (names only).
GET /v1/backups/{backend}/{id} -> read manifest: classes[] (collection names = the AI-scope proof), path (storage location/bucket), size, status.
GET /v1/schema -> enumerate live collection names, vectorizer, vectorIndexType, property names (schema-only sensitivity classification, no object reads).
GET /v1/nodes -> cluster/shard topology + stats (object/vector counts per shard) for scale, without reading any object.
Aggregate count only: GET /v1/objects?limit=0 or GraphQL Aggregate{meta{count}} to size the collection without pulling records.
restraint_note: Names-only. The backup_api_paths LIST and the manifest read (classes[], path, status, size) are the finding — enumerate metadata, do not exfiltrate. Forbidden: downloading a snapshot or its underlying objects/vectors (POST /v1/backups/{backend}/{id}/restore, or pulling the path/bucket contents) = exfiltration; POSTing /v1/backups (create) or /restore = a write/restore primitive and, given CVE-2025-67818, a potential arbitrary-file-write — never issue it. CVE-2025-67818/67819 are scoped/version-noted from the manifest only; do not weaponize. Blocked (401) = \"surface open, access not exercised,\" not a finding.
